retry_reason: The failure (NULL return) means the bus was not found in the list; this condition is transient if the bus is registered later, so retrying may succeed.
contract_summary: Get-wrapper: acquires a reference on the subsys_private structure associated with a given bus type by searching the bus_kset list. Returns the subsys_private pointer with an incremented refcount on success, or NULL if the bus is not found. The caller must call subsys_put (or equivalent) to release the reference when done. The function does not take any locks beyond the list_lock during the lookup; the returned reference is valid as long as the caller holds it and the bus remains registered. There is no automatic cleanup mechanism; the caller is responsible for balancing the get and put.

check.reason: The function 'bus_to_subsys' returns a struct subsys_private pointer after calling subsys_get(sp), which increments the refcount on the returned object. The access path 'sp.subsys.kobj.kref.refcount' correctly traces from the return variable (sp) through the nested structs: sp->subsys (struct kset) -> kobj (struct kobject) -> kref (struct kref) -> refcount (refcount_t). The type chain 'subsys_private--kset--kobject--kref--refcount_t' matches the actual struct definitions. Segment counts are equal (5 each), confirming the path is complete.
